documents.title: Addis Ababa City Administration And Civil Status
documents.content: The Addis Ababa City Administration is the executive organ of Ethiopia's capital. A mayor heads the administration; the lowest unit is the woreda, which has an elected council. Civil status services (birth, marriage and death registration) are provided at the woreda level. A birth certificate is obtained by registering a child's birth with the woreda, and a residence identity card is also issued at the same desk. The residence ID is required before obtaining a national passport.
documents.chunk_text: The Addis Ababa City Administration is the executive organ of Ethiopia's capital. A mayor heads the administration; the lowest unit is the woreda, which has an elected council. Civil status services are provided at the woreda level.

documents.title: Anbessa City Bus Routes
documents.content: Anbessa City Bus Service Enterprise is a state-owned operator in Addis Ababa. It was founded in 1945 and reorganised as a public enterprise in 1994. Key facts: Network size - 115 active bus routes. Route information includes bus number, origin, pass-by stops, destination and distance. Example: route 1 runs from Megenagna to Gurd Shola via Kara (7.7 km), route 2 runs from Kore Mekanisa to Addis Ketema via Lideta (11.1 km), route 3 runs from Ayertena to Minilik Square via Mexico (10.8 km). Passengers should consult the route table to find the correct bus number and distance before travelling.
documents.chunk_text: Anbessa City Bus Service Enterprise operates 115 active bus routes in Addis Ababa.

documents.title: Birth Registration And Certificate
documents.content: The Civil Registration and Residency Services Agency registers a child's birth at the woreda branch office and issues a birth certificate. The service covers live births and live births followed by immediate death; stillbirths are not registered. Eligibility: Proxy registration allowed if both parents are alive but unable to register due to force majeure. Death of a parent - when one parent has died, the living parent must present the legal death certificate. Persons with disabilities may register through their caregivers. Age of applicant - a person aged 18 or older must register their own birth. Required documents: Birth notification form from health facility, proof of guardianship if applicable, legal identification for abandoned children.
documents.chunk_text: Birth registration is provided at woreda level through the Civil Registration and Residency Services Agency.

documents.title: Birth Registration Service
documents.content:
# Birth Registration Service - Addis Ababa

## What is Birth Registration?
Birth registration is the official recording of a child's birth with the Civil Registration and Residency Services Agency (CRRSA). Every child born in Ethiopia has the right to a birth certificate.

## Where to Register
Visit the Civil Registration and Residency Services Agency branch office in your woreda (district).

## Eligibility
- All live births in Addis Ababa must be registered
- Parents can register their child if they are 18 years or older
- Proxy registration is allowed if parents cannot register due to force majeure

## Required Documents
- Birth notification form from the health facility (for hospital births)
- Written statement from the mother or father (for home births)
- Legal identification from police (for abandoned children)
- Proof of guardianship document (for children with guardians)

## Fees
- Current Registration: 60 Birr (Ethiopian citizens), 600 Birr (foreign citizens)
- Late Registration: 150 Birr (Ethiopian citizens), 750 Birr (foreign citizens)
- Expired Registration: 600 Birr (Ethiopian citizens), 900 Birr (foreign citizens)

## Contact Information
- Phone: 7533
- Email: info@aacrrsa.gov.et
documents.chunk_text: Birth registration service details for Addis Ababa residents.

documents.title: Broken Streetlight Guide
documents.content: Broken Streetlight Reporting Guide - Citizens can report a broken streetlight through the municipal infrastructure complaint desk. Required details include the street name, nearest landmark, and a short description of the issue. Reports can be submitted during office hours or through the official city complaint portal.
documents.chunk_text: Citizens can report broken streetlights through the municipal infrastructure complaint desk.

documents.title: Business Licence Application
documents.content: Businesses in Ethiopia must obtain a trade or business licence before operating and renew it periodically. Steps: 1. Prerequisites - Obtain a Commercial Registration Certificate, Tax Identification Number (TIN) and completed application form. 2. Fill out the application form. 3. Prepare required documents. 4. Submit the application to the relevant government office. 5. Obtain the licence - processing time and fees vary. Renewal: Licences must be renewed periodically (often annually or semi-annually).
documents.chunk_text: Businesses must obtain a trade or business licence before operating in Ethiopia.

documents.title: Business License Service
documents.content:
# Business License - Addis Ababa

## Overview
To operate a business in Addis Ababa, you need a business license from the Trade and Regional Integration Bureau.

## Where to Apply
Online: https://etrade.gov.et (Trade Portal)
In Person: Trade and Regional Integration Bureau offices

## Steps to Get Business License
1. Create account on trade portal
2. Register business name (get commercial registration)
3. Apply for business license
4. Submit required documents
5. Pay fees
6. Download certificate

## Required Documents
- Tax Identification Number (TIN)
- Passport-size photos (2)
- Copy of ID (passport, driver's license, or kebele ID)
- Power of attorney (if using representative)

## Fees
Fees vary based on business type and size. Check the trade portal for specific fees.

## Important
- Business license must be displayed in your shop/office
- License must be renewed annually

## Contact
- Trade Portal: etrade.gov.et
documents.chunk_text: Business license service details from Trade and Regional Integration Bureau.

documents.title: Digital Payment For Electricity Bills
documents.content: Ethiopian Electric Utility (EEU) encourages customers to switch from cash-based payments to digital payment options. This modernisation improves efficiency and reduces costs. Key points: Digital transition - EEU is shifting to digital payment methods to enhance transparency and save time. Digital Ethiopia initiative - The national programme aims to digitise service delivery across institutions by 2025. Payment options - Post-paid meter customers can pay monthly electricity bills using TeleBirr, CBE Birr, mobile banking and internet banking. Roll-out - Since May 2023, post-paid customers in Addis Ababa pay through digital options.
documents.chunk_text: Digital payment options for electricity bills include TeleBirr, CBE Birr, mobile banking and internet banking.

documents.title: Emergency Services Contact Numbers
documents.content: Addis Ababa emergency contact numbers: Police emergency - 991 or 922. Fire brigade - 939. Traffic police - 945. Ambulance services (Red Cross) - 907.
documents.chunk_text: Emergency contact numbers for Addis Ababa: Police 991/922, Fire 939, Traffic 945, Ambulance 907.

documents.title: Ethiotelecom APN And VoLTE Configuration
documents.content:
APN configuration for 5G/4G internet: Create a new Access Point Name with Name: Ethio Telecom and APN: internet. MCC: 636, MNC: 01 and APN type: default,supl. On Android: Settings > Connections > Mobile networks > Access point names, add new APN and restart.

VoLTE activation: Requirements - 4G/5G compatible device with VoLTE capability, latest SIM card and updated software. Activation steps: Settings > Connections > Mobile networks > enable VoLTE calls or Enhanced 4G LTE Mode; restart if prompted.
documents.chunk_text: APN and VoLTE configuration for Ethiotelecom customers.

documents.title: Ethiotelecom USSD Codes
documents.content: Ethiotelecom USSD codes: *127# - TeleBirr mobile money, *804# - check balance, *805# - recharge airtime, *806# - transfer airtime, *807# - call me back request, *809# - USSD services menu, *810# - credit services, *822# - CRBT services, *994# - SMS channel, *999# - Ethio Gebeta services. Short numbers: 822 - CRBT, 824 - voicemail, 903 - EVDO top-up, 909 - check balance, 980 - premium customer service, 700 - TeleBirr customer service.
documents.chunk_text: Ethiotelecom USSD codes for mobile services.

documents.title: Major Hospitals And Contact Information
documents.content: Major hospitals in Addis Ababa: Addis Cardiac Hospital - specialized cardiac hospital, phone: +251 11 6-298202. Saint Yared General Hospital - 50 beds, phone: +251 11 6464252. Landmark General Hospital - 30 beds, phone: +251 115-526292. Bethezata Hospital - 70 beds, phone: +251 11 5-514141/37. Bethel Teaching Hospital - 70 beds, phone: +251 11 3720122. Myung Sung Christian Medical Center - phone: +251 11 6295420. Tikur Anbessa (Black Lion) Hospital - phone: +251 11 5511211.
documents.chunk_text: Major hospitals in Addis Ababa with contact information.

documents.title: Marriage Registration Service
documents.content:
# Marriage Registration Service - Addis Ababa

## Overview
Marriage registration is the official recording of marriage with the Civil Registration and Residency Services Agency (CRRSA). Both civil and traditional/religious marriages should be registered.

## Where to Register
Visit the Civil Registration and Residency Services Agency branch office in your woreda.

## Eligibility Criteria
- Both parties must be 18 years or older
- Neither party can have an existing marriage
- Both parties must appear in person

## Required Documents
1. Birth certificates of both spouses (if available)
2. Two 4x4 photographs taken within the last 6 months
3. Divorce certificate (if previously married)
4. Proof of residence or national ID
5. Witnesses (if required)

## Marriage Notice Period
- Registrar posts notice for 15 consecutive days
- Objections can be submitted within this period

## Contact Information
- Phone: 7533
- Email: info@aacrrsa.gov.et
documents.chunk_text: Marriage registration service details for Addis Ababa.

documents.title: Marriage Registration Service And Certificate
documents.content: The marriage registration service is available at the woreda level through the Civil Registration and Residency Services Agency. Eligibility: Notification period - couples must notify the registrar at least one month before the intended marriage date. Public notice - the registrar posts a notice stating the marriage date for fifteen days. Objections - written objections can be filed within fifteen days. Minimum age - neither party may marry before age 18. Required documents: Identity documents, residence evidence, workplace marriage proof, witness identification, divorce certificate, photographs, witness appearance for religious/traditional marriages.
documents.chunk_text: Marriage registration eligibility and required documents.

documents.title: Residence ID Service
documents.content:
# Residence ID - Addis Ababa

## Overview
Residence ID is an official identification card issued to residents of Addis Ababa by the Civil Registration and Residency Services Agency (CRRSA).

## Where to Apply
Visit the Civil Registration and Residency Services Agency headquarters or branch offices.

## Eligibility
- Must be 18 years or older
- Must be registered as a resident in the woreda
- Must appear in person (cannot apply by proxy)

## Required Documents
1. Completed residence registration form
2. Two 4x4 passport photos (taken within 6 months)
3. Service request form

## Important Notes
- Residence ID is valid for the period specified on the card
- ID must be renewed before expiration
- Cannot be issued at business houses
- Diasporas and foreign nationals cannot get residence ID
- ID cannot be obtained through proxy

## Contact
- Phone: 7533
- Email: info@aacrrsa.gov.et
documents.chunk_text: Residence ID service details for Addis Ababa residents.

documents.title: Water Bill Payment Via Mobile Apps
documents.content: Water utility bills can be paid through the WeBirr platform using various mobile services. To pay with Telebirr: open Telebirr app, tap Payments > Service Payment or Utilities > select WeBirr or Water Utility, enter payment code and confirm. For CBE Birr Plus: open app, tap Pay > Pay Bill or Water Supply > choose WeBirr or water supply city, enter consumer code and confirm. Payments can also be made via USSD: dial *847# on CBE Birr or *127# on Telebirr, choose Pay Bill and WeBirr, enter payment code and confirm.
documents.chunk_text: Water bill payment options via mobile apps including Telebirr and CBE Birr.

documents.title: Smart City Digital Services Portal
documents.content: The Addis Ababa Smart City initiative provides digital services to residents through online portals. Services include: Online bill payments for electricity and water, Business license applications, Civil registration services, Complaint reporting, Transport route information, Healthcare appointment booking. Access portal at www.addisababa.gov.et or use mobile apps.
documents.chunk_text: Smart City digital services portal for Addis Ababa residents.

documents.title: Waste Management And Recycling
documents.content: Addis Ababa Waste Management: The city provides regular waste collection services. Residents should separate biodegradable and non-biodegradable waste. Recycling centers are available in major areas. Hazardous waste (batteries, electronics) should be disposed at designated collection points. Contact the municipal office for scheduled pickup times.
documents.chunk_text: Waste management and recycling services in Addis Ababa.

documents.title: Air Quality And Environmental Protection
documents.content: Addis Ababa Air Quality Monitoring: The city monitors air quality levels. High pollution days may trigger advisory alerts. Industrial areas are subject to emission regulations. Vehicle emissions testing is mandatory. To report industrial pollution, contact the Environmental Protection Authority.
documents.chunk_text: Air quality monitoring and environmental protection in Addis Ababa.

documents.title: Parks And Public Spaces
documents.content: Addis Ababa Parks and Recreation: The city has numerous parks and public spaces including Unity Park, Menelik Square, and neighborhood gardens. Some parks have jogging tracks, exercise equipment, and children's play areas. Park hours vary. Some parks charge a small entrance fee. Events may require permits.
documents.chunk_text: Parks and public spaces in Addis Ababa.

documents.title: Public Transportation Cards
documents.content: Anbessa Bus Service cards: Prepaid cards are available for regular commuters. Cards can be purchased at bus stations. Monthly passes offer discounted rates. Student and senior discounts available with valid ID. Lost cards can be replaced at main stations.
documents.chunk_text: Public transportation cards for Anbessa Bus Service.

documents.title: Tax Registration And TIN
documents.content: Tax Identification Number (TIN) in Ethiopia: Required for all businesses and some services. Apply at the nearest tax office or online via the tax portal. Required documents: Business registration, ID/passport, photos. TIN is free for individuals. Business TIN requires commercial registration. Tax returns must be filed periodically.
documents.chunk_text: Tax Identification Number registration in Ethiopia.

documents.title: Housing And Real Estate
documents.content: Housing in Addis Ababa: Various options from government housing to private apartments. Rent prices vary by area and amenities. Lease agreements should be registered. Real estate agents must be licensed. Property taxes apply annually. Commercial properties have different regulations.
documents.chunk_text: Housing and real estate information for Addis Ababa.

documents.title: Education And Schools
documents.content: Addis Ababa Education: Public and private schools available. Enrollment typically in August. Required documents: Birth certificate, vaccination records, photos. Some schools require entrance exams. International schools follow different calendars. Adult education programs available at community centers.
documents.chunk_text: Education and schools in Addis Ababa.

documents.title: Social Services And Support
documents.content: Social Services in Addis Ababa: The city provides various social support programs. Family protection services available. Elderly care programs. Disability support services. Job training programs. Food assistance for qualifying families. Contact the relevant woreda office for eligibility information.
documents.chunk_text: Social services and support programs in Addis Ababa.

documents.title: Digital Ethiopia 2025 Initiative
documents.content: Digital Ethiopia 2025: The national initiative aims to digitize government services by 2025. Key goals: Online service delivery, Digital ID systems, E-commerce support, Digital payments, Smart city infrastructure. Multiple government services now available online. More services being added regularly.
documents.chunk_text: Digital Ethiopia 2025 national initiative details.

documents.title: Green City Initiative
documents.content: Addis Ababa Green City Initiative: The city is working to increase green spaces and environmental sustainability. Tree planting programs available to residents. Solar energy incentives for homes. Electric vehicle charging stations being installed. Building energy efficiency standards being updated. Bike sharing programs launching in central areas.
documents.chunk_text: Green City Initiative for environmental sustainability.

documents.title: Metro And Light Rail
documents.content: Addis Ababa Metro (Light Rail Transit): The city has a light rail system connecting key areas. Two lines currently operational. Fares based on distance. Cards and mobile payments accepted. Operating hours: 6 AM to 9 PM. Priority seating for elderly and disabled.
documents.chunk_text: Addis Ababa Metro light rail transit information.

documents.title: Taxi And Ride Sharing Services
documents.content: Taxi Services in Addis Ababa: Yellow cabs (legal taxis) available. Ride-sharing apps operating in the city. Taxi stands at major locations. Agree on fare before departure or ask for meter. Special needs vehicles available upon request.
documents.chunk_text: Taxi and ride-sharing services in Addis Ababa.

documents.title: Small And Medium Enterprise Support
documents.content: SME Support in Ethiopia: Government programs support small businesses. Loan programs through development banks. Business training available. Market access programs. Export support for qualifying businesses. Technology and innovation grants. Contact the relevant bureau for current programs.
documents.chunk_text: Small and Medium Enterprise support programs.

documents.title: Tax Payment And Revenue Services
documents.content: Tax Payment in Ethiopia: Individual and business taxes are paid through the Ethiopian Revenue and Customs Authority. Methods: Online via tax portal, bank transfer, or in-person at tax offices. Monthly advanced tax for businesses due by 10th of following month. Annual tax returns due by 30th June. Penalties apply for late payment. Tax clearance certificate required for business licenses and permits.
documents.chunk_text: Tax payment methods and deadlines in Ethiopia.

documents.title: Property Tax Addis Ababa
documents.content: Property Tax in Addis Ababa: Annual tax on buildings and land. Rates vary by area classification (residential, commercial, industrial). Assessment done by City Revenue Office. Exemptions available for owner-occupied low-value properties. Payment due by end of fiscal year. Grace period with penalty for late payment. Updates done annually.
documents.chunk_text: Property tax information for Addis Ababa.

documents.title: Withholding Tax And VAT
documents.content: Withholding Tax in Ethiopia: Businesses must withhold tax on payments to contractors and suppliers. Standard rates: 2% for goods, 3% for services. Employers withhold income tax from employee salaries. VAT: 15% standard rate, some items at 0% or exempt. VAT registration required if annual turnover exceeds threshold. Monthly VAT returns due by 25th.
documents.chunk_text: Withholding tax and VAT in Ethiopia.

documents.title: Tax Incentive Programs
documents.content: Tax Incentives in Ethiopia: Export-oriented businesses may get income tax holiday. Small businesses in specified sectors enjoy reduced rates. Agricultural enterprises have special provisions. Manufacturing investments in priority areas get tax holidays. Application to Ethiopia Investment Commission required. Conditions apply and must be met.
documents.chunk_text: Tax incentive programs for businesses in Ethiopia.

documents.title: ID Card Renewal Process
documents.content: ID Card Renewal in Ethiopia: Residence IDs must be renewed before expiration. Process: Visit CRRSA office, submit renewal application, provide current ID, new photos. Fees apply for renewal. Processing time 2-4 weeks. Lost IDs require police report. Damage replacement needs explanation. Extensions available for those abroad temporarily.
documents.chunk_text: ID card renewal process in Ethiopia.

documents.title: National ID Application
documents.content: National ID (e-ID) in Ethiopia: The national electronic ID is being rolled out nationally. Required documents: Birth certificate or residence ID, fingerprints, photo. Application at designated centers. First IDs are free. Replacement costs apply. Links to other services gradually. Updates to personal information must be reported.
documents.chunk_text: National ID application in Ethiopia.

documents.title: Passport Application And Renewal
documents.content: Passport in Ethiopia: Ordinary passport application at Immigration Department. Requirements: Residence ID, birth certificate, photos, application form. Processing: Regular (30 days), urgent (7 days), super urgent (1-2 days). Fees vary by processing time. Validity: 5 years. Renewal requires current passport. Diplomatic passports separate category.
documents.chunk_text: Passport application and renewal in Ethiopia.

documents.title: Driver License Application
documents.content: Driver License in Ethiopia: Apply at Addis Ababa Transport Authority or regional offices. Requirements: Age 18+, medical clearance, training certificate, ID. Written exam and practical test required. Classes: 1 (motorcycle), 2 (car), 3 (truck), 4 (public transport). Validity 5 years. Renewal before expiration required. International driving permit available.
documents.chunk_text: Driver license application in Ethiopia.

documents.title: School Enrollment Addis Ababa
documents.content: School Enrollment in Addis Ababa: Public schools enrollment usually August. Required: Birth certificate, vaccination, photos, previous records (transfer). Zone-based enrollment for public schools. Private schools have own processes. Required immunizations: Polio, measles, etc. Free public education up to grade 8. Secondary education available. Special needs inclusion supported.
documents.chunk_text: School enrollment process in Addis Ababa.

documents.title: University Application Ethiopia
documents.content: University Application in Ethiopia: Higher education through Ethiopian universities or distance programs. Entrance exam required for federal universities. Application typically March-June. Private universities have direct admission. Requirements: Completed secondary school, entrance score. Fields: Engineering, Health, Business, Agriculture, etc. Scholarships available for top performers. Student loans possible.
documents.chunk_text: University application process in Ethiopia.

documents.title: Technical And Vocational Training
documents.content: TVET in Ethiopia: Technical and Vocational Education and Training available nationwide. Programs: craftsmanship, technician, other skills. Entry requirements vary by program. Short courses (3-12 months) and long courses (1-3 years). Government TVETs offer subsidized training. Private TVETs available. Industry partnerships for placement. Certificates awarded on completion.
documents.chunk_text: Technical and Vocational Training in Ethiopia.

documents.title: Adult Education Programs
documents.content: Adult Education in Ethiopia: Literacy programs available at community centers. Basic education for those who missed formal schooling. Distance learning options for adults. Evening classes in major areas. Free for qualifying participants. Certificates awarded. Contact woreda education office for locations and schedules.
documents.chunk_text: Adult education programs in Ethiopia.

documents.title: Waste Collection Schedule
documents.content: Waste Collection in Addis Ababa: Regular collection from residential areas. Schedule varies by sub-city. Collection usually early morning or late afternoon. Bin placement at designated points. Business areas have separate schedules. Holiday schedules may change. Missed collection reporting available. Recycling drop-off points at central locations.
documents.chunk_text: Waste collection schedule in Addis Ababa.

documents.title: Hazardous Waste Disposal
documents.content: Hazardous Waste Disposal: Batteries, electronics, chemicals require special handling. Designated collection points in the city. Chemical waste from businesses must use licensed contractors. Medical waste handled separately. Radioactive materials require special procedures. Never mix with regular waste. Contact Environment Authority for guidance. Fees may apply for certain items.
documents.chunk_text: Hazardous waste disposal in Addis Ababa.

documents.title: Industrial Pollution Control
documents.content: Industrial Pollution Control: Industries must follow emission standards. Regular monitoring required. Reporting to Environment Authority. Violations result in penalties or closure. New industries need environmental impact assessment. Green technology incentives available. Air quality standards enforced. Water pollution rules separate.
documents.chunk_text: Industrial pollution control in Ethiopia.

documents.title: Tree Planting And Urban Forestry
documents.content: Tree Planting in Addis Ababa: City-run tree planting programs for residents. Free tree seedlings available seasonally. Community planting events organized. School planting programs. Protected tree species require permit for cutting. Urban forestry initiatives expand green cover. Contact Environment Office for trees and permits.
documents.chunk_text: Tree planting and urban forestry in Addis Ababa.

documents.title: Rental Housing Process
documents.content: Rental Housing in Addis Ababa: Large rental market from compounds to apartments. Typical payment: 3-12 months advance. Lease agreements recommended. Verbal agreements have legal weight but written preferred. Eviction process requires court order. Utilities usually separate. Agent fees typically 1 month rent. Condition inspection recommended.
documents.chunk_text: Rental housing process in Addis Ababa.

documents.title: Property Registration
documents.content: Property Registration in Ethiopia: Land and property registration at Bureau of Land. Process: Application, survey, evaluation, title deed. Urban land lease from city. Freehold in some areas. Commercial properties have different process. Inheritance transfer requires court documents. Foreign ownership restricted. Timeline varies.
documents.chunk_text: Property registration in Ethiopia.

documents.title: Building Permit Process
documents.content: Building Permit in Addis Ababa: Required before construction. Application at City Planning. Requirements: Land title, architectural plans, structural design, environmental if required. Fees based on project size. Inspections at stages. Occupational permit needed after completion. Illegal construction penalties. Time to process varies.
documents.chunk_text: Building permit process in Addis Ababa.

documents.title: Real Estate Agent Regulation
documents.content: Real Estate Agents in Ethiopia: Must be licensed. Agent verification available. Commission rates typically 1 month rent for rental, 1% sale price. Agency agreements should be in writing. Client protection fund exists. Penalty for unauthorized practice. Contact Real Estate Council for complaints.
documents.chunk_text: Real estate agent regulation in Ethiopia.

documents.title: Tourism Sites Addis Ababa
documents.content: Tourism Sites in Addis Ababa: National Museum, Unity Park, Menelik Palace, Mount Entoto, Merkato market. Other sites nearby: Rift Valley, Langano Lake. Tourist guides available. Entry fees at most sites. City tours offered by licensed operators. History sites well-preserved. Opening hours vary.
documents.chunk_text: Tourism sites in Addis Ababa.

documents.title: Event Permits And Licensing
documents.content: Event Permits in Addis Ababa: Public events require permit from relevant authority. Application 2-4 weeks before event. Requirements: Venue, safety plan, security. Film permits separate process. Commercial events need additional licenses. Private events in homes usually no permit. Noise restrictions apply.
documents.chunk_text: Event permits in Addis Ababa.

documents.title: Cultural Events And Festivals
documents.content: Cultural Events in Addis Ababa: Ethiopian holidays: Meskel, Timkat, Enkutatash, Ethiopian New Year (13 September). Cultural festivals throughout year. Arts and music events. Film festivals. Food festivals. Major events at city squares and venues. Schedule available at tourism office.
documents.chunk_text: Cultural events and festivals in Addis Ababa.

documents.title: Hotel And Accommodation
documents.content: Hotels in Addis Ababa: International chains to local guesthouses available. Booking online or direct. Star ratings indicate amenities. Budget options in most areas. Serviced apartments for longer stays. Booking recommended. Peak season rates higher. Vacation rentals growing.
documents.chunk_text: Hotels and accommodation in Addis Ababa.
